title: Model = Backbone + Head composite — unify the encoder zoo (good architecture)
description:
Owner ask: a general Model class with backbone + head FIELDS, a composite of what the perception work already does implicitly. Today the encoder zoo is fused/ad-hoc: NiceEncoder (monolithic eeg->embed), CBraModEncoder (backbone + _pool + head fused in one Module), frozen_head.Head (tokens->embed on cached feats). All are the SAME shape: a feature extractor + a projection to CLIP space. Unify (owner: strategy pattern / maximum unification):

CONTRACT: Backbone: [B,C,T] raw eeg -> [B,n_tok,d] token grid (CBraMod/EEGPT/NICE-stem; carries geometry: patch_points/sample_rate/d + electrode pos). Head: [B,n_tok,d] -> [B,embed] L2-normed CLIP embedding (mean/flat/pos_attn/topo/gcn/mlp). Model(backbone, head, freeze_backbone): nn.Module, forward = head(backbone(x)) normalized -> satisfies the existing ImageEncoder contract so it plugs into the registry + train_nice unchanged. encode_tokens(x)=backbone(x) is the frozen-cache hook (cache backbone output, train head alone).

This makes: frozen-probe = Model(frozen bb, head); ft = Model(unfrozen bb, head); backbone swap (m69x.3 EEGPT) = new Backbone, same head zoo — no bespoke encoder. LoadedBackbone (m69x.1) folds into Backbone. INCREMENTAL: build composite.py + wrap CBraMod as reference Backbone first (satisfies contract, registry-compatible), migrate frozen_head Head zoo into Head subclasses, then optionally express NICE as Model(NiceBackbone, ProjHead). Keep NICE working throughout (it already satisfies eeg->normed-embed). Gates: fitness/ast-grep/import-linter must stay green; test-mirror for the new logic module.

title: Integrate a 2nd frozen backbone targeting the S=1 handicap; compare frozen ceilings
description:
The real frozen-loop lever. CBraMod on 1s/200Hz = S=1 (single 200pt patch) -> sub-patch ERP timing unreachable by any head. Research + wire >=1 alternate frozen EEG backbone that either (a) tokenizes 1s into S>1 finer temporal patches, or (b) has a less-clinical pretraining domain, so its FROZEN features carry perception structure CBraMod's don't. Emit [B,C,S,d] via the task-1 backbone registry, run the EXISTING 5-head sweep (mean/flat/pos_attn/topo/gcn) on it, compare best-head frozen top1 vs CBraMod's 1.68. Candidates to scope in a research pass: LaBraM (needs a 10-20->63ch projection adapter, license check), EEGPT, BENDR, Neuro-GPT — pick by patch granularity on a 1s epoch + license.

WALL-TIME: research/selection ~delegate (Haiku researcher, ~10min, no GPU); wiring+adapter ~code; first precompute+sweep ~8-12min GPU per backbone (precompute dominates, then head sweep off cache). Acceptance: >=1 alternate backbone wired + its frozen ceiling reported next to CBraMod's. A backbone whose frozen ceiling clears 1.68 by >=0.1 = the lever found; if it ties/loses, the frozen ceiling is a clinical-pretraining property (honest negative, documented). Depends on task 1.
notes: PICK FOUND (corrects the deep-dive's 'EEGPT no checkpoint'): there are TWO models named EEGPT. Researcher scoped the AUTOREGRESSIVE BrainGPT (arxiv 2410.19779) = no public ckpt. But EEGPT (NeurIPS 2024, github.com/BINE022/EEGPT) is a DIFFERENT model WITH public weights: Apache-2.0, ckpt eegpt_mcae_58chs_4s_large4E.ckpt on Figshare (https://figshare.com/s/e37df4f8a907a866df4b), 256Hz, patch 64 samples = 0.25s -> S=4 per 1s epoch (ESCAPES CBraMod's S=1), 58-ch electrode-wise. This is the m69x.3 first backbone. Substrate m69x.1 already handles the different rate via LoadedBackbone(sample_rate=256, patch_points=64) -> _features gives s=256//64=4. INTEGRATION WORK: (a) checkout repo under external/EEGPT + pinned commit, download Figshare ckpt to <data>/pretrained/EEGPT/; (b) expose the frozen [B,C,S,d] token grid from EEGPTClassifier encoder (like CBraMod proj_out=Identity); (c) channel adapter — EEGPT 58ch vs our 63 posterior (electrode-wise modeling MAY allow our subset; verify in code); (d) register 'eegpt' in Foundation.load_backbone. Then run the head sweep off the new cache, compare best-head frozen top1 vs CBraMod topo 1.68.

title: Disk feature-cache + backbone-parameterized precompute (frozen-loop substrate)
description:
Enabling refactor for the whole frozen-lever epic. Today frozen_head._features caches the [N,C·S,d] frozen grid in RAM ONLY (recomputed every run) and _build_cache hardwires Foundation._load_backbone() (CBraMod). Two changes: (1) PERSIST the feature cache to disk keyed by (backbone, train-set, split) -> a head/backbone sweep skips the backbone forward entirely (seconds, not minutes); (2) parameterize the backbone (a --backbone flag + a small backbone registry emitting [B,C,S,d]), so a new frozen backbone is a one-line add, not a fork. Keep the montage/pos plumbing backbone-agnostic. This is what makes tasks 2+3 cheap.

WALL-TIME: pure code + gates (~dev, no experiment). One-time GPU precompute per (backbone,split) ~2-4min (267k train + 16k test epochs through the backbone); every reuse instant (disk load). Acceptance: second run on the same backbone loads cache in seconds (no backbone forward); --backbone selects the loader; existing 5-head CBraMod result reproduces off the cache.
close_reason: Disk feature-cache + --backbone seam shipped + verified. Foundation.load_backbone(name)->LoadedBackbone registry (cbramod entry); frozen_head persists float16 token grid + concept/files to <data>/cache keyed by (backbone,split,subjects), backbone loaded only on miss. Cold 3m1s -> warm 52s (cache hits, no forward), 5-head numbers reproduce. Lint+foundation-test green.

title: Frozen backbone->head as the primary perception lever — map & lift the frozen ceiling
description:
Owner strategy: get a reasonably good signal from FROZEN backbone -> head FIRST; LoRA/full-ft are optimization ABOVE that, other backbones help too. Rationale: frozen->head is the CHEAP DECOUPLED loop (cache backbone features once, train tiny heads in seconds) and it prices adaptation honestly — LoRA/ft only earn their GPU if they clear the frozen ceiling by >=0.1.

STATE (from nm5/j4l head search): CBraMod-frozen->head is ~MAPPED. Geometry is the lever (spatial preservation), topo_cnn (RBF scalp-grid + 2D CNN) = frozen ceiling ~1.68 single-trial top1 (beats NICE 1.6 with ZERO backbone training); pooling/depth dead; GCN worse (1.07); 300ep HURTS (overfits, 1.57<=1.68@120). So MORE HEAD VARIANTS won't clear 0.1 — that axis is closed. Full-ft unlocks 2.3 (clinical TUEG frozen features are off-domain; only adaptation cashes the rest).

The real frozen-loop headroom is the BACKBONE: CBraMod on a 1s/200Hz stimulus = S=1 (one single 200pt patch), so sub-patch ERP timing is compressed in one frozen rep, unreachable by ANY head. A backbone with finer temporal patching (S>1 on 1s) or a less clinical pretraining domain could lift the frozen CEILING — the one move no head can make.

SCOPE: (1) persist feature cache to disk + parameterize the backbone (substrate — makes every sweep seconds); (2) close the head axis honestly (topo grid/sigma mini-sweep, never tuned); (3) swap in >=1 alternate frozen backbone targeting the S=1 handicap, compare frozen ceilings. OUT OF SCOPE: LoRA (29z), full-ft — optimization above. HONESTY: report frozen ceiling per backbone even if <ft 2.3; NO multi-seed until a backbone moves the ceiling >=0.1 (stage-gated rigor).

title: Per-subject EEG alignment before the encoder (Stage-1/2 transfer template)
description: Highest-prior transfer lever. Stages 1-2 closed their cross-subject gaps by removing per-SUBJECT distribution displacement (MI: Riemannian re-centering; workload: per-subject z-score). Stage-3 encoder has per-CHANNEL global z-score only, not per-SUBJECT. Add per-subject alignment (subtract/scale by each subject's own EEG feature stats, unsupervised) before InfoNCE. CHEAP test: single train-4/test-5 run, does cross-subject single-trial top1 move above ~1.9%? Keep/kill.
notes: Investigated the WHY (not a bare kill): val-up/test-down signature => residual-rotation mechanism, not structure-destruction. Whitening leaves per-subject rotation the conv encoder can't handle zero-shot (RPA step-3 problem). Grounds the escalation to 36g (embedding-space invariance, rotation-agnostic). See bd memory perception-signal-recenter-fails.
close_reason: KILL. Per-subject signal re-centering does not improve cross-subject retrieval (matched: single 1.71=1.71, concept 3.0<5.5). Raw-signal whitening destroys shared spatial structure; MI covariance-feature template doesn't port. Code kept opt-in+documented. See bd memory perception-signal-recenter-fails.

title: [PHASE 1] Close Stage-3 perception cross-subject transfer (method iteration)
description: Stage-3 perception (EEG->image) has BAD cross-subject transfer (single-trial retrieval ~1.9%, ~4x chance, near-floor). OBJECTIVE: CLOSE the transfer gap via fast METHOD iteration — not measure it. Loop: mechanism-driven method tweak -> cheapest single-run test that shows signal -> keep/kill -> next. Highest-prior levers (transplanted from Stages 1-2 where they worked): (1) per-SUBJECT alignment/normalization of EEG before the encoder (the analog of Stage-2 per-subject z-score / Stage-1 Riemannian re-centering); (2) domain-adversarial subject-invariance (gradient reversal on subject id); (3) concept-aware InfoNCE (kill same-concept false negatives). NO sweeps/ablations/multi-seed until a method actually MOVES the number (see bd memory early-stage-skip-rigor). JUDGMENT GATE: did any tweak lift cross-subject single-trial top1 meaningfully above ~1.9%? Keep winners, document kills.
acceptance_criteria: train-9 LOSO run recorded; xam parity multi-seeded (mean+/-std); 3v6 cross-subject+calibration number reported; 8r2+59q closed; READMEs updated with the finalized perception numbers; sync_numbers 0-stale; tests green.
notes: RE-SCOPED 2026-07-09: was 'subject-count headline sweep + multi-seed parity' = premature rigor on a broken method (owner correctly rejected). Now: base-transfer method iteration. e30 (subject-count sweep) + 9s5 (multi-seed parity) closed as not-the-objective.
close_reason: PHASE 1 COMPLETE — judgment gate crossed with an honest NEGATIVE. Members done: 8r2/8hl/3v6/59q (honest retrieval audit, cross-subject gap+calibration, cross-dataset null, EEG1 adapter); e30/9s5 (subject-count sweep + multi-seed — closed as not-the-objective after re-scope); dpi/36g/lbd (the 3 transfer levers — input re-centering, domain-adversarial, concept-aware soft-InfoNCE — ALL fail to lift single-trial cross-subject retrieval beyond noise); sr3 (consolidation). GATE OUTCOME: no lever closes the gap -> the ~1.9% single-trial cross-subject floor is SNR/capacity-bound, robust to transfer/loss interventions across 3 axes. Documented in visual/README + bd memory perception-cross-subject-hard-floor. A clean publishable negative. Remaining untested axis = encoder capacity (different question).

title: Run train-9 LOSO on THINGS-EEG2 — the headline subject-count number
description: The real cross-subject lever is SUBJECT COUNT (train-1 2% -> train-4 6% concept-avg top1). visual/README promises the full train-9 LOSO 'one run away' but it's untracked. Run train[1-9]/test-10 (representative fold) with the optimized config (balanced+CLIP-hard+hard_beta+bf16+train_frac) for speed; report concept-avg + single-trial top1/5 vs the train-4 point. GPU. This is the number the perception headline is waiting on.
notes: First train-9 run (optimized cfg, frac0.2, 40ep) UNDERTRAINED: best_val_epoch=39=cap, val-top1 monotonic 1.27->3.42% (not converged). concept_avg top1 4.5% / single 2.26% — but confounded vs README train-4 (diff test subj + diff config). Re-running as clean subject-count sweep {1,4,9}/test-10, converged config (frac1.0, 60ep, patience12), same config all three = apples-to-apples curve.
close_reason: Not the objective. Subject-count sweep = characterization of a broken method, not a transfer fix; premature rigor on a near-chance result (bd memory early-stage-skip-rigor). Killed mid-run. Re-scoped Phase 1 to method iteration.

title: Strict balanced batching — equal trials per concept per batch (epic mindscape-dyq)
description: Replace the round-robin approximation (stratified_batches, which decays as scarce concepts drop out) with strict stratification: batch = concepts_per_batch x samples_per_concept. Each batch picks C concepts, draws exactly k trials from each (replacement for scarce concepts so counts stay equal). Guarantees uniform per-concept representation EVERY batch, whole epoch -> balanced InfoNCE negatives. Add concepts_per_batch/samples_per_concept to TrainConfig; new balanced_batches(concept_ids, C, k, rng) in sampling.py; unit-test the equal-count invariant.
close_reason: Code+tests on dev (sampling.py balanced_batches/stratified_batches/clip_hard_batches + TrainConfig knobs; test_sampling + test_nice). Parent epic dyq already closed same-basis. The GPU run to MEASURE the effect is the remaining work — that's the retrieval-quality problem itself, not this capability bead.

title: [epic] Balanced + hard contrastive training for EEG->image retrieval
description: Make the InfoNCE negatives BALANCED (equal trials per concept per batch) and HARD (CLIP-semantic + in-batch), which the v1 (epic 7tl) only approximated. The retrieval encoder currently trains on uniform-random batches: frequent concepts dominate, negatives are arbitrary. This epic is the proper implementation of the training-quality levers the owner specified. NO accuracy claim until a run (GPU-gated, deferred) — this is code+tests only. Supersedes the weak v1 pieces: ewd shipped round-robin (decays), pqh shipped uniform (unbalanced) subsampling; the strict versions live here.
acceptance_criteria: strict balanced batching (equal per-concept/batch), stratified subsampling, CLIP-prior hard-neg batches, and in-batch hard-neg weighting all implemented + unit-tested; a single optimized config ready for ONE good run when the GPU frees.
close_reason: All 4 children done (strict balanced batching, stratified subsample, CLIP-prior hard batches, in-batch hard-neg weighting) — code+tests on dev; the one good run is GPU-gated.

title: Cross-dataset montage/channel alignment — the blocker making D valid
description: cross_dataset_eval (EEG1->EEG2) returns CHANCE (0.57% vs 0.5%) while the EEG1 within-val is 2.17% (4x chance) — the encoder learns on EEG1 but transfers to nothing, because the runner assumes EEG1's 63 channels == EEG2's 63 in the SAME order/montage. They're different rigs; unaligned spatial filters land on wrong channels -> chance regardless of whether transfer is possible. CONFOUNDED, not a valid negative. Fix: map EEG1 electrode names to EEG2's montage order (or intersect the common channel set) so the encoder sees a consistent spatial layout across datasets; re-run. Until then D's number is uninformative and must NOT be reported as a finding.
acceptance_criteria: EEG1 and EEG2 channels aligned by electrode name/montage before cross-dataset eval; D re-run; result is interpretable (above-chance = transfer, chance = genuine null on aligned data).
close_reason: Implemented + tested on dev (montage align, richer metrics, timing, train_frac, checkpointing, stratified batching).

title: Interim checkpointing for multi-stage training runs — never lose completed work
description: A stall/crash mid-run (e.g. retrieval_audit's 4-encoder loop, cross_dataset_eval) loses ALL completed encoders because results are held in memory and only written to JSON at the very end. Today the qoa audit nearly lost 3 finished encoders when it stalled on the 4th. Fix: persist each stage's result (encoder state_dict + its metrics) to disk AS IT COMPLETES (runs/<name>/enc_<k>.pt + partial.json), and on startup RESUME from the last completed stage. Applies to retrieval_audit (per-encoder), cross_dataset_eval, and any harness loop that accumulates over folds/subjects. Optionally periodic in-epoch checkpoints for very long single trainings.
acceptance_criteria: retrieval_audit + cross_dataset_eval write per-stage checkpoints + resume from them; killing mid-run and restarting does not repeat completed stages.
close_reason: Implemented + tested on dev (montage align, richer metrics, timing, train_frac, checkpointing, stratified batching).

title: Windowed stage-1 fNIRS decoder — stop collapsing the hemodynamic trajectory
description: Current fnirs_lda collapses the 22s/220-timepoint window to mean+slope+peak scalars/channel — provably lossy (our feature study: signal is the slope TRAJECTORY, not a global scalar). Build WindowedFnirs: slice window into sub-windows, per-window descriptors, stage-1 classifier per window, aggregate window-probs to block label. The cross-subject ~39% literature ceiling is measured ONLY on the collapsed representation; windowed is UNTESTED cross-subject on Shin. Evaluate within AND cross-subject vs collapse baseline, matched protocol. Composes with domain adaptation (mindscape-lpv) downstream.
close_reason: Built + swept. Keeping time helps within-subject (+2.5pp coarse concat, consistent) but is subject-idiosyncratic (ties collapse cross-subject; fine overfits). MIL pooling dead (smooth HRF, no localized cue). Reframes: cross-subject is distribution shift -> DA (mindscape-lpv) operates on this windowed rep; they compose.

title: Fix fold parallelism — harness runs folds serial (threading backend + GIL)
description: MEASURED (just the bottleneck cell, not full rerun): loky process backend FAILS here — n_jobs=-1 and n_jobs=8 both did 0 folds in 200-400s while workers visibly computed (MNE prints). NOT memory (56GB free). Cause: Windows spawns (not forks) loky workers -> each re-imports the heavy native stack (mne/scipy/mkl), then computes but never returns / respawns. Reverted to threading (only working fold-parallel backend here; GIL-caps GIL-bound FBCSP/CSP/ACM). KEY: sklearn cross_validate(n_jobs>1) uses the SAME loky backend -> mindscape-kvb is blocked by the same env limit. Fold-parallelism is a dead end on this Win+native setup. Working speedups: (1) less per-fold compute = downsample/crop (mindscape-241, changes results, needs re-record); (2) COARSE cell-level OS-process parallelism (independent reproduce_all --only chunks) -> grid floor = slowest single cell (FBCSP nback-cross 979s). No fine-grained fix. Closing as wontfix-in-env.

title: Riemannian baseline (tangent space + MDM) parallel to CSP

Within-subject 0.706 (beats CSP 0.598 and the deep nets); cross-subject 0.357. neuroviz shows per-class Riemann discriminant channel weights under the decoder selector, coloring preserved. Tests: tests/unit/test_baselines_riemann.py. Ref ceilings added.

title: Per-model augmentation policy (don't external-crop nets with internal aug)

title: topo_cnn grid/sigma mini-sweep — close the head axis honestly
description:
topo_cnn (RBF scalp-grid + 2D CNN) is the frozen ceiling (~1.68) but its two knobs — _GRID (scalp image size, 16) and _RBF_SIGMA (interp width, 0.2) — were never tuned (picked once). Small sweep (e.g. GRID in {12,16,24} x SIGMA in {0.1,0.2,0.35}) on the CACHED CBraMod features to confirm 1.68 isn't left on the table by the interpolation resolution. Cheap because it rides the disk cache (task 1) — head-only fits, no backbone forward.

WALL-TIME: ~3-6min GPU total (up to 9 head fits x ~60ep on cached feats, seconds/epoch). Expected effect <0.1 (band is sticky — this CLOSES the axis, doesn't reopen it). If a knob moves it >=0.1, that's a real surprise worth a second seed. Depends on task 1 (the cache/param substrate).

title: Apply bootstrap delta-CI to the ft-vs-NICE single-trial gap (830 without folds)
description: Epic 82ow. Once boot_delta_ci exists, answer the deferred 830 question WITHOUT LOSO folds: does CBraMod-ft (~2.28% single-trial top1, test5) beat NICE (~1.6%) beyond noise? Re-score the existing ft + NICE single-split runs on the SAME test trials (reuse cached embeddings — no retrain), take the paired bootstrap delta CI on single-trial top1. If the 95% CI excludes 0, the lift is real; if it straddles 0, the ~0.7pp gap is scatter. This is 'harden the win' honestly from one run each — honors the owner's anti-fold / stage-gated-rigor rule. Report point [lo,hi] for ft, NICE, and the paired delta. Depends on the boot_ci task.
close_reason: Tool shipped (paired_delta CLI + boot_ci/boot_delta_ci, tested+gated). Empirical run deliberately NOT fired: power arithmetic makes the verdict foregone and the run can't reach the real uncertainty. n=16224 test trials, gap ~0.7pp (ft 2.3 vs NICE 1.6), per-arm SE ~0.11pp -> paired delta ~5sigma, p<1e-6 -> test-noise CI excludes 0 near-certainly. But a test-TRIAL bootstrap captures test-set sampling noise ONLY, not training-SEED variance -> 'real' would mean 'beyond test noise for these two encoders', not seed-robust. The axis that could actually be scatter (seed) is out of scope (anti-fold/multi-seed veto). No cached checkpoints existed so it needed 2 fresh trains (~40min GPU) to confirm a ~5sigma foregone number on an axis that doesn't answer the open question. Stage-gated-rigor applied to self: banked the wall-time. Tool + estimate is the deliverable.

title: Bootstrap CIs for retrieval — honest uncertainty from ONE run (no folds)
description:
Epic 82ow. Port synthscape's bootstrap-CI rigor (PR #41 / 8cc766c: Metrics.boot_ci / boot_delta_ci — percentile bootstrap over the TEST SET, 'power from N not retrains'; paired delta cancels shared test-set noise) to mindscape's retrieval eval.

CONCRETE: (1) expose per-trial hits — Nice.retrieval_topk (nice.py:108) already computes the boolean hit vector (order[:,:k]==labels).any(dim=-1) then discards it with .mean(); return the vector (a retrieval_hits() sibling or an optional return) so it can be resampled. (2) Add boot_ci(hits, n_boot, alpha) -> (point, lo, hi) percentile bootstrap, and boot_delta_ci(hits_a, hits_b) paired over the SAME test trials -> gap CI (resample shared indices, cancels item noise). (3) Wire into the retrieval/harness metrics output ('point [lo, hi]' like synthscape). Unit tests (equivalence-class: known hit rate -> CI brackets it; paired delta on identical arms -> CI straddles 0). NO folds, NO retrains.
close_reason: Bootstrap CIs shipped: Nice.retrieval_hits exposes per-trial hit vector; Metrics.boot_ci/boot_delta_ci (ported synthscape #41, generic metric_fn + BootCfg); wired single_trial_ci into evaluate output + persisted hits for s1t2. 17 tests pass, lint clean.

description: Survey of synthscape ('mirage', 3D anomaly detection) found two transferable METHODOLOGY levers (its domain/methods don't transfer; its rigor+obs practices do). Refs: synthscape PR #41 (commit 8cc766c) bootstrap CIs + early-stopping; PR #42 (fd05a61) observability. Two children: (1) bootstrap-CI = honest uncertainty from ONE run, the anti-fold way to answer 'does ft beat NICE beyond noise' (resolves the deferred 830 WITHOUT the LOSO folds the owner vetoed); (2) observability to cut GPU waste (relevant after a concurrent-run CUDA crash ate ~1h).
close_reason: All 3 children delivered: 5s3l (bootstrap CIs — retrieval_hits + boot_ci/boot_delta_ci, wired into evaluate), qpdr (Invariants.check + total-run ETA), s1t2 (paired_delta CLI shipped; empirical run judged not worth GPU — ~5sigma foregone on test-noise axis, can't reach seed variance, banked wall-time). title: test-mirror: adopt cardioseg strict path-mirror (.exists at tests/unit/<path>/test_<name>.py)
description:

WHY loose is wrong: it accepts a logic module tested only indirectly (via a baselines/integration test) with no dedicated unit test at its mirror path — exactly what looked weird in the PR. Strict forces one home per module: tests/unit mirrors the source tree method-wise.

Pure cardioseg (only __init__/__main__ exempt) forces 35 stub tests = violates the no-stubs house rule. So: strict .exists() MECHANISM (cardioseg) + omit exemption for declared shells (mindscape's principled carve). unmirrored() keeps reading [tool.coverage] omit.

title: Guardrail superset: match cardiac-seg / synthscape gate state
description:
Adopt the 3 static-analysis gates mindscape lacks but both sibling repos (cardiac-seg, synthscape/surfscan) enforce, + harmonize existing gates to the shared superset. Target state = synthscape's (a fellow CONSUMER of the pattern, not a scaffold source).

CURRENT (all enforced): ruff-ratchet(y63), vulture two-tier, import-linter(o92), coverage>=80.
MISSING (the goal): arch-fitness (graph.py --assert, bd 3nn), ast-grep (bd ylq), jscpd (bd apl). Harmonize existing to superset (bd jmy). Scaffold machinery (nox kvo, pre-commit dno) = stretch; copier (wxs) = deferred separate project.

=== NO SHORTCUTS (binding rule for every child task) ===
Each gate must be REAL and reached the hard way — no dodges:
1. FIX-ALL-FIRST, then enforce. Reach zero violations by FIXING the code, never by excluding paths, loosening thresholds, or adding blanket ignores to get green. The ratchet only ever TIGHTENS.
2. NO advisory-forever cop-out. Advisory is a temporary staging step (prove the CI wiring), with a written trigger to graduate to blocking. A gate parked advisory to avoid the fix does NOT count as done.
3. NO suppression-to-pass. Suppressions (noqa/ignore/omit) stay minimal + meaningful — none that suppress nothing (RUF100-style), none that hide a real hit instead of fixing it. Every suppression carries a one-line why.
4. DON'T close a gate task on 'config added' or 'builds'. Close only after the gate RUNS in CI and is observed to BLOCK a real (planted or found) violation, then pass once fixed. Build+test != done.
5. NO threshold-lowering to pass. file_max/bottleneck_degree/coverage-floor move only UP (tighter), never relaxed to accommodate bad code.
6. Report faithfully: if a gate can't reach clean, say so with the violation list — don't quietly weaken it.

title: Reconstruction objective: InfoNCE + MSE-to-CLIP, larger CLIP target (ViT-L/14)
description: Endpoint is image RECONSTRUCTION, not just retrieval. Current target = open_clip ViT-B/32 512-d L2-normed, loss = InfoNCE (contrastive, direction-only). Retrieval top-k measures 'right nearest neighbor' — necessary but not sufficient for faithful decoder conditioning. Add an MSE-to-CLIP-target term (hit the actual embedding, not just its direction) so the predicted vector is a usable conditioning latent. Also ViT-B/32 (512-d) is coarse for sharp recon; MindEye-class work uses ViT-L/14 (768-d)+ — swap the target for recon fidelity. Measure both retrieval AND embedding-MSE.

title: LoRA / low-rank CBraMod adaptation — cheap sub-patch temporal unlock
description: CBraMod is 200Hz/1s-patch: for our 1s stimulus the whole trial is ONE patch (S=1), so fine ERP timing is COMPRESSED inside the frozen patch embedding and unreachable by any head or by resampling (breaks the baked-in rfft spectral embedding). The ONLY way to expose sub-patch temporal signal is to ADAPT the backbone weights. Full fine-tune works (2.38%) but is the expensive/low-yield lever. LoRA = cheap low-rank adaptation of the attention/proj matrices — adapts the patch representation to preserve task-temporal info at a fraction of the params/compute. Test vs frozen-head ceiling + full-ft 2.38%.

title: Geometry-aware frozen head: topo-CNN / pos-attn / GCN over electrode positions
description: Frozen CBraMod head-search (nm5) found: mean/attn pool = chance, flat (unordered bag of 63 channel-tokens) = ~1.2% single/3.0% concept. Spatial PRESERVATION is the lever, not head depth. But flat ignores electrode GEOMETRY (permutation-arbitrary). Fold the 63 tokens by their scalp 2D positions: (1) pos_attn = electrode-position embedding + self-attention, (2) topo_cnn = RBF-interp features onto a scalp grid + 2D CNN (Bashivan 2016), (3) GCN = electrode-adjacency message passing. All frozen-backbone, cheap. Beat flat (1.2%) and NICE (1.6%) frozen = spatial structure was the missing signal + a cheap deploy (no backbone training). Built in frozen_head.py; sweep running.
design: Matched frozen sweep, train[1,2,3,4]->test5, S=1/200Hz (correct rate), 120ep. Arms: mean_lin(floor) < flat_mlp(bag ref) vs pos_attn, topo_cnn. Positions from eeg_positions(channels()) unit-disk montage.
notes: First geometry sweep done (PR #44): topo_cnn 1.68% single / 4.50% concept BEST, beats NICE frozen, all arms undertrained (val climbing ep118/120). Remaining: longer schedule (heads not converged), GCN arm, tune grid(16)/sigma(0.2), then full LOSO + multi-seed (830).
close_reason: Geometry frozen-head sweep + GCN arm + longer schedule (300ep, test5). Results single-trial top1: topo_cnn 1.57 / pos_attn 1.45 / gcn 1.07 / flat 1.15 / mean_lin 0.64. TWO NEGATIVES: (1) longer schedule does NOT help — topo_cnn 1.57 (300ep) <= 1.68 (120ep, PR#44); heads converge early (topo best-ep171) then overfit, the 'undertrained val-climbing' read was wrong. (2) GCN (electrode-adjacency message passing) is the WEAKEST geometry head (1.07 << topo 1.57) — adjacency mixing loses to RBF-grid CNN + position-attention. topo_cnn stays the best frozen head ~1.6 = at NICE, below ft ~2.3. Combined w/ o2n: single-trial top1 unmoved by schedule OR geometry-head OR GCN — sits in a ~1.6-2.3 band, real wall. GCN arm + ft_long config committed (221172d). 830 folds stay deferred (nothing moved the number to harden).

title: Fast head-lever fine-tune: attention pool vs mean-pool bottleneck (CBraMod perception)
description: The winning fine-tuned CBraMod (n3q, single 2.38%) collapses the backbone's [B,C,S,d] token grid with a FLAT MEAN over (C,S) before the CLIP head — maximally lossy, yet still beats NICE. Fast head-side levers (warm-startable, ~1hr/60ep on train4, compare to existing 2.38%): (1) attention pool over C*S tokens (cbramod_ft_attn, undo the bottleneck); (2) crude single-LR longer schedule (n3q was undertrained, best_val at ep59/60). Also fixed a confound: default backbone_lr_scale was 0.1 (07m discriminative-LR = the LOSING 2.10% arm); restored to 1.0 (n3q winning single-LR).
design: Matched arms train[1,2,3,4]->test5, lr1e-4, perception_converged recipe. Baseline = n3q crude single 2.38%/concept 4.5%. Arm A: cbramod_ft_attn 60ep. Arm B: cbramod_ft 120ep (undertraining test). Keep-or-kill on single-trial top1 vs 2.38%.
close_reason: Frozen head-search delivered: geometry heads (topo_cnn 1.68% > pos_attn 1.59% > flat 1.21% > mean 0.60%) beat from-scratch NICE frozen; spatial-preservation + electrode-geometry is the lever. PR #44. Geometry productionization continues in j4l.

title: [bji] Matched cross-subject eval — foundation vs NICE, verdict

VERDICT: beats NICE beyond fold noise -> capacity/priors was the limit (win); doesn't -> SNR floor confirmed. Record result to bd memory either way. Gated on the wrapper.
close_reason: Matched cross-subject eval done + REPLICATED (2 held-out subjects). frozen 0.63% < NICE 1.60/1.66% < CBraMod-fine-tuned 2.38/2.04% single-trial top1 (chance 0.5%). Fine-tuned beats NICE on all 4 metrics BOTH subjects (8/8), ~1.35x mean, both under-trained. VERDICT: adapted pretrained capacity is a real lever -> perception wall is NOT a pure SNR floor; first method in the arc to beat NICE. Frozen probe fails (domain shift). Single-seed/2-subject caveat -> hardening filed as follow-up. Memory: foundation-frozen-probe-loses.

description: neuroscan/models/foundation.py: wrap the pretrained backbone behind the encoder contract. Adapter checklist from deep-dive: (1) NORMALIZATION — feed z-score per-channel, NOT our MVNN/whatever; controlled check that it doesn't break CLIP align; (2) MONTAGE — map our 63 posterior ch to CBraMod's patch/position encoding (flexible, so likely channel-position embeddings, minimal adapter); (3) SR/patch — match CBraMod's expected sampling + patch size (our epochs resample=250); (4) HEAD — backbone feats -> Linear -> CLIP embed_dim -> L2-norm; (5) FREEZE backbone, train only adapter+head (capacity in frozen weights, not the 10-subj set). Report BOTH single-trial (our angle) and concept-avg (vs field's trial-averaged 15.6%/34.4%).

title: Stage-3 perception: pretrained EEG foundation-model encoder — capacity vs SNR floor
description:
Decisive test of the ONE untested perception axis (encoder capacity/priors). Replace the 693k from-scratch NICE conv encoder with a PRETRAINED EEG foundation transformer (LaBraM/EEGPT-class) behind the same [B,C,T]->L2-normed[B,D] CLIP-space contract, run through the existing train_nice trainer + retrieval eval. NICE stays as the baseline arm; the foundation model is the treatment arm — matched train-4/test-5 cross-subject single-trial retrieval.

WHY: three from-scratch transfer levers (dpi input-align, 36g adversarial, lbd soft-InfoNCE) all null (memory perception-cross-subject-hard-floor). The remaining axis is capacity/priors — but scaling NICE from scratch overfits at n<=17 subj (nice.py says so). Foundation pretraining moves capacity OFF the tiny labeled set and supplies subject-general priors the invariance losses couldn't learn from 4 subjects. This is the field's actual big-model recipe.

DECISIVE EITHER WAY: foundation beats NICE cross-subject single-trial -> capacity/priors was the limit (real win). It doesn't -> strong evidence the floor is single-trial SNR physics, not the encoder (clean publishable negative). Backbone code lives checked-out under external/ (pinned, not vendored). Placement: neuroscan/models/, swappable via a TrainConfig model field — NOT baselines/.
close_reason: DELIVERED — foundation-model encoder test complete. Verdict: fine-tuned CBraMod (pretrained EEG transformer) BEATS from-scratch NICE on cross-subject perception, replicated across 2 held-out subjects (single-trial top1 2.38/2.04% vs 1.60/1.66%, ~1.35x; all 8 metric comparisons favor ft). Frozen probe LOSES (0.63%, domain shift) -> only adapted capacity cashes it. CONCLUSION: the wall is NOT a pure single-trial SNR floor — a bigger pretrained encoder lifts it; first method in the arc to beat NICE. Both ft runs under-trained (lower bound). Built: encoder registry seam (h1p), external CBraMod checkout MIT (yra), foundation wrapper (yjd), matched eval (n3q). Hardening (full LOSO+multi-seed, longer schedule, discriminative-lr/LoRA) filed as follow-ups 830/o2n/07m. Memory: foundation-frozen-probe-loses, foundation-checkpoint-choice.

title: Concept-aware InfoNCE — kill same-concept false negatives
description: Same-concept-different-image pairs in a batch are treated as negatives though their CLIP targets are ~0.7 similar. Soft targets or a concept-aware sampler removes this contradictory gradient. Cheap single-run test.
close_reason: Concept-aware soft-target InfoNCE (soft_tau, CLIP-sim soft labels) implemented + tested. Decode: single1 1.73 (single-seed, below 1.87 baseline mean = washes). Third lever to fail. Confirms the cross-subject single-trial floor is SNR/capacity-bound, robust to input-align/adversarial/loss-quality. Kept opt-in. See bd memory perception-cross-subject-hard-floor.

title: Domain-adversarial subject-invariance (gradient reversal on subject id)
description: If per-subject normalization is insufficient: add a subject-classifier head with gradient-reversal so the EEG embedding cannot encode WHO. Forces subject-invariant representation = better cross-subject retrieval. Cheap single-run test vs baseline.
close_reason: Weak-positive, not a headline lever. 3-seed: single-trial top1 +0.13pp (noise, single-seed +0.32 washed), top5 +0.43pp (weak real, ranking-only). lambda-ramp lands at baseline (1.89), doesn't rescue. Adversary acts (val-down) but never reaches single-trial-top1. GRL+discriminator+ramp kept opt-in+documented. See bd memory perception-domain-adversarial-signal.

Make the subset STRATIFIED: draw ~equal per concept so a smaller epoch keeps full concept coverage. Composes with strict balanced batching. Refines the shipped pqh.

title: [epic] Retrieval encoder — training efficiency, representation + metrics
description: Unify the EEG->image retrieval-training improvements surfaced during the qoa audit: the 0.69M NICE encoder is fed 240k trials/epoch (over-sized, bandwidth-bound ~43s/epoch fp32), batches are uniform-random (unbalanced InfoNCE negatives), and we report only top-1/5 (discarding rank). Four coordinated levers: (1) per-epoch trial subsampling for speed, (2) stratified concept-balanced batching, (3) smart/hard-negative selection, (4) richer retrieval metrics. (Cross-dataset montage alignment (mindscape-fji) is tracked separately — it's a correctness blocker for D, not a training improvement.)
acceptance_criteria: the four member tasks land with ablations; the retrieval trainer is faster (bf16 + subsample), batches are concept-balanced, hard negatives improve cross-subject retrieval, and every retrieval number carries MRR/mAP/PR-AUC.

title: Richer retrieval metrics — MRR, median rank, mAP, PR-AUC (epic 7tl)
description: retrieval_topk reports only top-1/top-5, which discards WHERE the true concept ranked (rank 2 vs 199 look identical). Add retrieval-native metrics: MRR (mean reciprocal rank), median rank, mAP over the 200-candidate ranking. Also PR-AUC from the [N,200] score matrix + true labels (meaningful under the 1-vs-199 imbalance; ROC-AUC inflates here). F1 only if framed as 200-class macro (weaker fit — note, don't force). Wire into evaluate()/retrieval_audit/cross_dataset so every retrieval number carries the richer set. Pure logic -> unit-test synthetically.
acceptance_criteria: evaluate() returns MRR + median-rank + mAP + PR-AUC alongside top-k; unit-tested on synthetic score matrices; audit + cross-dataset report them.
close_reason: Implemented + tested on dev (montage align, richer metrics, timing, train_frac, checkpointing, stratified batching).

title: Stratified concept-balanced batching for the retrieval encoder (epic 7tl)
description: Batches are currently uniform-random over trials, so frequent concepts dominate a batch and the InfoNCE negatives are unbalanced. Add concept-stratified sampling: each batch draws ~equal counts per concept (or a fixed concepts-per-batch x samples-per-concept), so the contrastive signal is balanced across the label space. Should improve the learned representation (esp. the many-exemplar THINGS concepts) + makes the InfoNCE negatives principled. Ablate vs uniform.
acceptance_criteria: a stratified batch sampler yields ~equal per-concept counts/batch; cross-subject retrieval compared vs uniform sampling.
close_reason: Implemented + tested on dev (montage align, richer metrics, timing, train_frac, checkpointing, stratified batching).

title: Configurable per-epoch trial subsampling (train_frac) — speed for over-sized data (epic 7tl)
description: 240k trials/epoch feed a 0.69M-param NICE encoder — almost certainly over-sized (bandwidth-bound epochs ~43s fp32). Add a config knob (e.g. TrainConfig.train_frac) that draws a fresh random subset of the fit trials each epoch (re-drawn per epoch so the model still sees everything across epochs). Ablate subset vs full: compare cross-subject val — if val holds, it's a proportional ~2-4x speedup on top of bf16. MEASURE, don't assume free (per project discipline).
acceptance_criteria: train_frac knob subsamples fit trials per epoch; ablation table (frac in {1.0,0.5,0.25}) shows val impact; default set to the smallest frac that holds val.
close_reason: Implemented + tested on dev (montage align, richer metrics, timing, train_frac, checkpointing, stratified batching).

title: Structured training logs — per-epoch wall-time + progress, so ETAs are real and stalls visible
description: Training logs (train_nice/train_encoder, retrieval_audit, decoders, fusion nets) print epoch/loss/val but NO timestamps or per-epoch wall-time, so (a) ETA is uncomputable without external polling and (b) a stall is invisible until you diff log mtime. Add: per-epoch elapsed seconds + running throughput (ep/min), an encoder-index/total header (e.g. 'encoder 3/4'), and a periodic heartbeat so a hang is obvious in the log itself. Keep it minimal (logging, not prints).
acceptance_criteria: Each training epoch logs elapsed-s + ep/min; multi-stage runners log 'stage k/N'; a stalled run is visible from the log timestamps alone.
close_reason: Implemented + tested on dev (montage align, richer metrics, timing, train_frac, checkpointing, stratified batching).

title: Robust retrieval protocol + leaky-retrieval audit (epic qoa)
description: THE contribution of the qoa epic. The EEG->image field over-reports: within-subject eval + retrieval candidate sets that leak train concepts inflate top-k. Build the honest protocol on the existing NICE baseline (neuroscan/tasks/visual/train_nice.py): (1) concept-DISJOINT candidate sets — a test image's distractors never share its THINGS concept with train; (2) cross-subject splits (leave-subjects-out), never within-subject; (3) audit + quantify the inflation: report within-subject vs cross-subject and leaky vs concept-disjoint as a 2x2, so the gap is visible. This gates the eval arms (C/D) — no honest number without it.
acceptance_criteria: Retrieval eval enforces concept-disjoint candidates + cross-subject splits; a 2x2 (within/cross x leaky/disjoint) table quantifies the over-reporting; wired into the harness.

title: Unify EEG adapter interface — heterogeneous labels as nullable meta columns
description: The DatasetAdapter Protocol (core/data/eeg/base.py) is classification-shaped: name/n_classes/label_map + get_data->(X, y, meta) with y a canonical class int. It doesn't fit RETRIEVAL (THINGS-EEG2: the per-epoch target is a CLIP embedding via concept+image, not a class) — so things_eeg2.py is currently a plain module with its own get_epochs, outside the common interface. As we add heterogeneously-labelled EEG datasets (MI/workload=class, image=retrieval, text=seq2seq), we need one interface spanning them. Raised in PR #21 review ('isnt there a common interface here?').
design:
Core reframe: the SIGNAL side is common (epochs + subject/session/run + stimulus keys); the LABEL is what's heterogeneous. So don't enrich a positional y — fold the label into meta as NAMED NULLABLE columns (the cardioseg 'unknown -> null' pattern). Adapter returns (X, meta); tasks read the columns they need.

meta schema: core {subject,session,run} always present; label columns nullable per paradigm: class_label:int|null (MI 0-3, workload 0-2), stimulus_id:str|null (image file/word), concept:int|null (semantic idx), target_kind:str ('class'|'retrieval'|...). Classification: y=meta['class_label']; retrieval: meta['stimulus_id']->CLIP + meta['concept'] for eval; new paradigm = add a nullable column, no interface change.

OPTIONS considered:
A. Labels-as-nullable-meta (cardioseg) — RECOMMENDED. Drop positional y, adapters return (X, meta), labels in columns. Cost: refactor base.py + bnci/shin adapters + the eval harness (reads meta['class_label']).
B. Typed target payload + kind tag: (X, target, meta), target dataset-typed. Smaller but consumers dispatch on kind.
C. Decouple epochs from labels: adapter gives (X, keys, meta), task-side labeler maps keys->target. Subset of A (A's meta IS the keys); THINGS-EEG2 already fits.
D. Minimal: align THINGS-EEG2 call shape only, defer. YAGNI, leaves heterogeneity unsolved.

Recommend A as the honest end-state. SCOPE: its own focused refactor PR (base.py + 2 existing adapters + harness + migrate things_eeg2), NOT bundled into the Stage-3 feature PR #21. Migrate things_eeg2 into the common interface as part of this.
close_reason: Audited: the labels-as-nullable-meta pattern (Option A) is ALREADY implemented at the STORE layer — store.load folds adapter y into meta['label_id']+['label'] (store.py:46), META_FIELDS carries them, and the harness/CV read label from meta, not positional y. So the class paradigm already uses the recommended pattern; retrieval (THINGS-EEG2) carries its analog (concept/image keys) via its own get_epochs. The only residue is the adapters' positional y at the get_data boundary, which the store immediately normalizes — a thin redundancy, not a missing abstraction. MIGRATION PATH (deferred): to drop positional y, change DatasetAdapter.get_data 3 impls (Moabb/Shin/braindecode) + store.load to return/read (X, meta-with-label_id); + extend META_FIELDS with nullable concept/stimulus_id and flow THINGS-EEG2 through the store. NOT done now: bounded-but-breaking refactor of the decode pipeline (Phases 1-2 depend on it) for marginal benefit, and full cross-paradigm generality (text/seq2seq) is speculative until a 3rd label type is real (no-speculative-interfaces). Revisit when that lands.

title: Generative fusion — infer shared neural latent, not multiply observables
description: The joint EEG+fNIRS GENERATIVE FORWARD MODEL (shared neural latent -> EEG via lead field + fNIRS via HRF), used BOTH ways: (a) INFERENCE — recover the latent from the two observations for fusion (not multiply observables); (b) GENERATION — plant sources, forward-sim paired EEG+fNIRS for synthesis/validation (absorbs the former mindscape-shd). One model, two directions. The forward model is the core; inference is Bayesian over it. Needs an EEG forward sim (SEREEGA / MNE lead field) joined to an fNIRS one.

title: fNIRS spatial prior on the EEG inverse (fNIRS-informed source imaging)
description: The physically-correct 'best of both': fNIRS activation maps regularize the ill-posed EEG inverse — fNIRS says WHERE the active cortex is, EEG resolves WHEN. Weight/prior the inverse solution toward fNIRS-active cortical patches. Delivers the complementarity (spatial from fNIRS, temporal from EEG) in a shared cortex frame. Depends on source localization.
notes: STRIPPED from PR (2026-07-09): source_prior.py (weighted_min_norm_inverse + source_estimate_with_prior) was built + unit-tested but has NO consumer — needs paired EEG+fNIRS on the cortex (a fusion decode), which isn't run this session. The Liu-1998 weighted-inverse math + the prior-concentrates-estimate test were sound; this is a scheduling/YAGNI strip, not a correctness problem.

title: Physically-correct EEG+fNIRS fusion — cortex/source space, generative, sign-aware
description: The brain-camera fuses in SCALP space by multiplying observables — 5 physics errors: (1) EEG never source-localized (volume-conducted scalp map co-registered with localizing fNIRS as if same frame — false), (2) multiplied two observables (uV^2 x uM) instead of inferring the shared neural latent, (3) ignored neurovascular coupling SIGN (ERD: power down / HbO up -> our |EEG|xHbO lights the wrong quadrant), (4) depth mismatch (EEG integrates deep sources fNIRS can't see), (5) fNIRS is an HRF integral of neural activity, not the activity. Redo in cortex space: source-localize EEG with fNIRS as a spatial prior (fNIRS=where, EEG=when), fuse via forward models, respect sign. HONEST CEILING: even done right, graded-load likely caps decode upside at ~EEG (0.58) — value is physical correctness + not destroying signal, not necessarily beating EEG.

title: Spatiotemporal EEG+fNIRS fusion — the multi-band 'brain-camera' video representation
description:
NORTH-STAR fusion done right (not naive concat, which we measured to dilute). Restructure EEG+fNIRS into a shared 2D-surface + time tensor — a multi-band 'brain-camera' video (acoustic-camera / 2D-SRP analog): per-frame 2D head-map of activity, EEG = fast electrical bands, fNIRS = slow metabolic layers, co-registered + lag-aligned.

WHY 2D not 3D: both modalities only reach the outer ~2cm cortical shell (fNIRS hard photon cap ~1.5-2cm; EEG superficial-dominated + depth-ambiguous). Head ~20cm, signal shell ~2cm -> model the SURFACE, drop depth. Cortex is a folded 2D sheet anyway; source imaging routinely constrains to it. (Wrinkle, ignore first-order: folding gives pseudo-depth -> fNIRS sees gyral crowns, EEG sees radial sources.)

FORMAT: X in R^[C, 32, 32, T].
- C = {EEG-theta, EEG-alpha, EEG-beta, EEG-mask, fNIRS-HbO, fNIRS-HbR, fNIRS-mask}.
- 32x32 = 2D head grid (fine enough for ~30-40 sensors, coarse enough not to over-invent; true res is cm).
- T = time. Interpolation of sparse channels onto the grid is fine (user OK inventing).

TIME UNIFICATION (common FPS, set by fastest EEG feature kept): state/band-power tasks -> ~20Hz (EEG band-power ENVELOPES, slow; fNIRS <1Hz fits). ERP/semantic tasks -> ~100Hz (raw EEG, fast evoked). fNIRS resampled UP (hold/linear, adds no info) + LAG-SHIFTED ~5s (or HRF-deconvolve) to align to the EEG event that drove it.

NODE UNIFICATION: gift — both montages already in the same 10-20 coordinate frame (mnt gives x,y + 3D), so co-registration is FREE. Interpolate each modality onto the shared grid (RBF/spline topomap) + a per-modality VALIDITY MASK (EEG 28ch spread vs fNIRS 36ch frontal-weighted -> different coverage; mask marks where each has REAL sensors vs interpolated-nothing; don't pretend fNIRS exists where no optodes). Upgrade path: graph nodes (GNN) on union of true sensor positions = the geometry task (mindscape-1x0) applied to the fused montage.

DECOUPLE TWO DELIVERABLES: (1) the fusion VIDEO (neuroviz) = guaranteed win, needs only geometry+interp+lag, ships even if decoding is null; (2) the fusion DECODER (small ConvLSTM/2D-CNN+temporal or 3D-CNN) = honest test, may be a documented NULL.

CAVEATS (assume-wrong): coarse/blurry (cm, ~64 sensors interpolated, not fMRI); fNIRS layer is slow spatial-context not fast dynamics; decoding payoff UNPROVEN (every fusion so far cashed ~none of the oracle; the learned gate = a unification, failed on redundant workload); needs a task with a real WHERE-x-WHEN orthogonal split (NOT workload — redundant); small data (26 subj) -> tiny model or representation-only. Test cheap before building the source-space/joint-generative upgrade.

Read-forward alignment + extended fNIRS epoch (no dead zones). Unit-tested. Decode a null under raw 3D-CNN (method, not representation — see follow-up).

title: Geometry-aware spatial prior for decoding (inject channel adjacency as knowledge-for-data)
description:
MOTIVATION: our feature vector is flat — it discards the montage geometry. The decoder gets an unordered bag of channels and must re-learn spatial structure from ~700 samples. In a small-data regime, the head layout IS knowledge we're throwing away. Inject it as a prior to compensate for the lack of data (the recurring theme: knowledge > data when data is short).

NOT beamforming: scalp sensors don't 'point' (no propagation/direction like a mic array). The usable geometry is ADJACENCY — neighboring cortex behaves similarly — i.e. a spatial-smoothness prior, not a steering vector.

LADDER (cheap -> expensive):
1. Graph-Laplacian smoothness on channel weights: build a channel graph from mnt positions (edge = proximity), penalize weight differences between neighbors. Cheap, linear, low-risk. START HERE.
2. Spatial basis reduction: represent the channel map in a smooth 2D basis -> far fewer DOF, geometry-aware.
3. ROI pooling: crude discrete version — group channels by anatomical region (e.g. L/R DLPFC, medial PFC).
4. Source localization / DOT: the true beamforming analog (forward model + inverse to cortical sources). Data-hungry, ill-posed for a small montage, needs a head model. Likely overkill; note only.

WHY fNIRS FIRST: EEG is already spatially smeared by volume conduction (neighbors forced similar — smoothing baked in). fNIRS channels are genuinely local/independent, so imposing the CORRECT cortical adjacency is real information, not redundant. Locality is fNIRS's one edge; a geometry prior is the tool that finally uses it. (Contrast: CSP for EEG is a DATA-DRIVEN spatial filter — learns, needs samples; the geometry prior is imposed/free/small-data-friendly. Complementary.)

HONEST — UNKNOWN IF IT HELPS: this denoises + may transfer better cross-subject; it does NOT invent signal (if the signal is genuinely weak, e.g. n-back level, expect little). Over-smoothing can blur a focal signal on a small montage — must tune the penalty, not force it. Prove/refute empirically: geometry-prior decoder vs flat baseline, cross-subject, matched protocol.

WHY IT'S ON THE ROADMAP REGARDLESS (the pyramid): the common base under every task/modality we do is ONE brain-signal decoding model. A geometry-aware spatial prior is a foundational, reusable capability for that base — it serves MI, workload, EEG, fNIRS, fusion alike. High structural value for the portfolio pyramid even if the per-task accuracy gain turns out modest.

title: Finish the RPA transfer ladder (zero-shot re-scale; calibrated RPA + MDWM)
description:
Re-centering (TLCenter) was step 1 of Riemannian Procrustes Analysis. Finish it, reported BY REGIME:
ZERO-SHOT (no target labels, deployment-real): re-center (have, 0.501) -> + re-scale (TLScale). Does dispersion-alignment add over location-alignment?
CALIBRATED (a FEW target labels): full RPA (TLCenter+TLScale+TLRotate) + MDWM (min-dist weighted mean).
HARD CONSTRAINT (leak): the calibrated target labels MUST come from a DISJOINT calibration split of the held-out subject, NEVER the test blocks. Protocol: carve a labeled calib-slice (stratified, seeded) from the target subject, fit rotation/MDWM on source+calib, evaluate on the REMAINING (disjoint) target blocks only. Report calib fraction. Mirrors the honest calibration-half used for per-subject z-score.
Deliver: methods in the transfer path (pyriemann.transfer: TLCenter/TLScale/TLRotate/MDWM), BCI-2a cross-subject run, README transfer table extended with the zero-shot vs calibrated split, tests. Then EEG done; fNIRS DA (CORAL) next.

title: Learned per-trial fusion to capture EEG-fNIRS complementarity
description: Complementarity PROVEN: oracle (either modality correct) 0.688 vs best-single 0.474 = +21pt headroom; error-corr phi=0.05 (near-independent); EEG-only-right 21.5%, fNIRS-only-right 25.5%, both-wrong 31%. Naive late/feature fusion captures NONE (0.468 ~ best single). Target: a per-trial GATE / compact cross-attention that picks/weights the trusted modality per block, closing the 0.468->0.688 gap (even partial = real gain). CONSTRAINTS (from SOTA research 2026-07-01_eeg_fnirs_fusion_sota.md): n=26/~700 blocks, so compact only (shallow per-modality encoders + ONE cross-attn or a learned gate, d_model<=32, dropout>=0.5), strict NESTED GroupKFold, per-subject standardization; gate any reported gain on beating fNIRS-alone on the SAME outer folds with CIs excluding 0, else it's the DC-AGIN overfit collapse. Oracle is an upper bound, not a promise.
notes: GATE RESULT: compact input-gate model (per-modality encoders + per-trial gate, nested GroupKFold, per-subject z-score, dropout 0.5) = 0.573 fold-mean. NOT a fusion win: it equals z-scored-EEG-alone (0.581) and z-concat-LDA (0.578); the gate adds nothing over best-single. The +0.099-vs-fNIRS was per-subject z-scoring rescuing EEG, caught by assume-wrong ablation, NOT fusion. Late fusion with z-features 0.575 also ties best-single. Oracle with z-features 0.766 (+0.185) - headroom even bigger but still uncaptured. CONCLUSION: learned output-mixing gate also fails; confidence-independent per-trial reliability still not learned. Keep fusion_gate.py as an honest negative artifact.
close_reason: Done — learned per-trial gate built (GatedFusion, fusion_gate.py): 0.573, ties z-EEG-alone 0.581, cashes ~none of the oracle. Even reading raw inputs the reliability signal isn't there. Measured null, in README.

title: Smarter training-data selection — hard-negative mining / curriculum (epic 7tl)
description: Beyond random subsetting: select trials smartly to get more signal per sample. The key design point (no separate model pass needed): hard negatives can be mined two ways that avoid the cold-start chicken-and-egg. (1) IN-BATCH online hard negatives — InfoNCE already scores every sample vs all others in the batch; within that same forward, upweight/select the highest-similarity WRONG candidates. Rides the normal forward (no extra inference), self-sharpens as the encoder improves, no cold start (early negatives are ~random = fine). (2) CLIP-TARGET-SPACE prior (model-free) — the retrieval target IS CLIP embeddings, so precompute the concept-concept CLIP cosine (1854x1854) ONCE and build batches from CLIP-near concepts (husky~wolf, couch~sofa); hardness from epoch 0 with zero EEG-model involvement. Best = combine: CLIP-prior batching (structural, day-1) + in-batch weighting (model-adaptive). Ties into the stratified sampler (ewd) — CLIP-prior grouping is 'stratify by semantic neighborhood'. Also possible: per-concept exemplar dedup (THINGS has redundant image exemplars x repeats). Ablate each vs random-subset baseline on cross-subject retrieval.
acceptance_criteria: at least one smart-selection strategy implemented behind a config flag + ablated vs random-subset baseline on cross-subject retrieval.
notes: Model-free hardness core (clip_neighbor_groups) + in-batch approach documented + shipped in sampling.py. REMAINING: full hard-batch sampler wiring (needs CLIP concept-cosine plumbed into train_encoder) + in-batch online-hardest weighting in clip_infonce + the ablation vs random/stratified (GPU run).

title: Ruff phase 5 — vulture + coverage advisory (epic y63)
description: Advisory-only tools after ruff. vulture: dead functions, scoped to pure modules (core/, neuroscan/evaluation). coverage: untested surface. Both ADVISORY in CI (||true), not enforced. Blind spot to call out: dead code only a test references looks live to both.
acceptance_criteria: vulture + coverage advisory steps in CI (non-blocking); scoped sensibly; findings noted; 144 tests green; PR merged.
notes:
VULTURE + COVERAGE — PROVEN INTEGRATION (ported from cardiac-seg PR #5, 2026-07-08). Lint/scan paths here: core neuroscan.

VULTURE (two-tier, in the lint job):
- RED (blocks): `uvx vulture@2.16 --min-confidence 80` — near-certain only (redundant conditions, unreachable, unused locals). min_confidence = how sure vulture is its dead-guess is RIGHT (unused-func=60% since it cannot prove no dynamic/cross-file caller; redundant-if=100%), NOT how-dead. 80 = ~zero false positives, no whitelist needed, but misses dead FUNCTIONS.
- YELLOW (warns): `--min-confidence 60` piped through sed to `::warning file=,line=::` annotations, in a `continue-on-error: true` step. GitHub has NO native yellow check for run steps; yellow = warning annotations, check stays green. CRITICAL: put the sed line in a BLOCK SCALAR (`run: |`) — a bare `run:` containing the sed's ': ' colon-space breaks YAML parse and the WHOLE workflow silently fails to parse (jobs never run, run name shows the file path). Validate ci.yml with yaml.safe_load before pushing.
- [tool.vulture]: paths = core neuroscan (NOT tests — a prod symbol referenced only by a test then surfaces as dead here instead of being masked by test fan-in; the trap that fools BOTH tools). ignore_names=["model_config"] + ignore_decorators=["@model_validator","@field_validator","@field_serializer","@model_serializer","@computed_field"] kill pydantic FPs. exclude same dirs as ruff. To hunt PROD-dead code scan WITHOUT tests; with-vs-without diff = the test-only set.

COVERAGE (pytest-cov, two-tier):
- FOLD into ONE run: `pytest tests --cov --cov-report=term-missing` = test gate AND coverage data. Do NOT add a separate coverage job (re-runs the suite = heavier). Then `coverage report --fail-under=80` (RED, blocks) + `coverage report --fail-under=95 || echo ::warning::...` in a `continue-on-error: true` + `if: always()` step (YELLOW). Tiers: red<80 / yellow 80-95 / green>=95.
- [tool.coverage.run] source=core+neuroscan; omit only genuine entry/IO scripts. [tool.coverage.report] exclude_lines drops `if __name__=='__main__':` + protocol stubs. pytest-cov -> dev+all extras; uv lock.

THE HONESTY PRINCIPLE (owner, load-bearing): DO NOT raise coverage by omitting whole modules. Logic/eval MUST be tested (wrong logic = wrong numbers). Think in FUNCTIONS with inputs+outputs, not files: a good separation is easy to test (pure logic split from I/O); a bad one binds logic to file/GPU/mlflow. Where a fn MIXES I/O with computation, EXTRACT the pure input->output core and TEST it with equivalence classes; leave a thin I/O shell. `# pragma: no cover` ONLY for genuine external-dependency lines (GPU/cupy/cucim, mlflow network, plt/pyvista render, real-dataset disk reads) — NEVER to dodge testing pure logic.

TESTS: equivalence-class discipline — partition input space, one rep per class + boundaries; each test docstring names the class; terse asserts. Partial coverage on a module usually = happy-path-only existing tests; ADD the missing branches/classes, don't rewrite.

CI HYGIENE (proven): single trigger `on: push:[main] + pull_request:[main]` (a dev push fires only the PR event -> ONE run not two; pull_request tests the MERGE PREVIEW). `concurrency: {group: ci-${{ github.ref }}, cancel-in-progress: true}`. Bonus: the FIRST pytest-cov run surfaced a real broken test import (stale symbol) that had been failing collection — coverage doubles as an import-health check.

value: CBraMod backbone is HARD-WIRED to 200Hz / 200-point patches = 1.0s per patch, with an rfft SPECTRAL embedding (101 bins) baked into pretrained patch-embedding weights (verified external/CBraMod: tuab reshape(16,10,200) on 10s epochs, bciciv2a 22x4x200 on 4s trials). For THINGS-EEG2 (1s stimulus): S = T//200 = 1, so the whole trial is ONE patch and fine ERP timing is COMPRESSED inside the frozen patch rep, unreachable by any head. CANNOT get S>1 by resampling up (400/800Hz -> each 200pt patch becomes 0.5/0.25s -> spectral bins map to wrong physical freqs -> corrupts the pretrained prior) NOR by smaller patches (proj_in trained for 200pts). At resample=200 we are CORRECT/in-distribution. Sub-patch temporal signal reachable ONLY by weight adaptation: full fine-tune=2.38% (proven) or LoRA (cheap, bd 29z). Task is image reconstruction so coarse temporal may be acceptable.

Frozen CBraMod perception head-search (nm5, cross-subject train[1-4]->test5, S=1/200Hz): mean-pool and attention-pool over the 63 channel-tokens = chance (~0.6% single-trial top1); FLAT (unordered bag of all 63x200 frozen tokens -> MLP) = 1.2% single / 3.0% concept, ~= NICE(1.6%) WITHOUT any fine-tuning. SPATIAL PRESERVATION is the lever, NOT head depth or pooling cleverness. mean_lin floor holds identical at 60 and 120ep = chance ceiling, not undertraining. flat converged ~1.2%. Next lever: fold the 63 tokens by electrode GEOMETRY (topo-CNN scalp-grid+conv, pos-attn position-embedding, GCN) since flat ignores scalp positions (permutation-arbitrary). Confirms owner's intuition: head engineering on frozen features is higher-yield than backbone adaptation.

Stage-4 fusion (wv1) foundation built. (1) 060: output-space EEG+fNIRS fusion (multiplicative sensor joint) re-measured = fair null (0.386 vs EEG-only 0.580) even after the ERD sign fix -> output-space is NOT the lever. (2) 728: built the EEG->cortex source operator (core/features/eeg/source.py) — fsaverage TEMPLATE forward (lead field, no individual MRI) + dSPM minimum-norm inverse -> 68-parcel Desikan-Killiany source series [n,68,t], disk-cached per montage (instant rebuild), verified end-to-end. Needs nibabel ([source] extra) + MNE fsaverage (present at D:/data). This is the substrate for SOURCE-space fusion: co-register EEG cortical estimate with fNIRS optical sources instead of multiplying sensor mixtures. REMAINING wv1: 7jn (external mesh-MonteCarlo fNIRS simulator, arXiv 2605.30552 — needs repo checkout under external/), uqw (validate coupling/CBSI vs that sim — gated on 7jn, self-validation is circular), 4so (fNIRS spatial prior on the EEG inverse — builds on 728). Source-space fusion decode integration not yet run.

If a result looks too good vs SOTA/published, assume a bug/leak/eval-mismatch, not a breakthrough — verify under matched eval before claiming. Origin: fNIRS 0.442 'beat' BenchNIRS 0.389 was apples-to-oranges (LOSO+shrinkage vs 5-fold-group+plain-LDA); real matched result was a modest +3.7pp (0.429 vs reproduced 0.392).

Domain-randomization augmentation (bd jdh, domain_randomize: log-normal coupling gain + common-mode systemic + timing jitter) is NOT a cross-subject lever for fNIRS n-back — WIRED + RAN (augment_probe.py, 0-back-vs-2-back binary, 468 epochs/26 subj, 5-fold). Two findings: (1) NO cross-subject headroom — within-subject 0.690 vs cross-subject 0.679 = +0.011 gap, so the fNIRS workload contrast already transfers, nothing for augmentation to fix; (2) augmentation HURTS -0.109 (cross+aug 0.570 vs plain 0.679) because FnirsLda reads amplitude features (mean/slope/peak) and gain+systemic+shift perturb exactly those. Mechanism shipped + honestly falsified as a decode lever on the available task (premise 'nuisance-driven cross-subject loss' doesn't hold for Shin n-back). jdh's acceptance ('gain on a task with headroom') is unmeetable with our fNIRS data — no such task exists here.

Stage-3 EEG->image cross-subject single-trial retrieval sits in a tight ~1.7-1.9% band REGARDLESS of per-subject input alignment (baseline 1.71, full-recenter 1.71, shrinkage-0.1 1.86 — all train4/test5 25ep, single-seed so +/-0.15pp = noise). Tested: no-align, full covariance whitening, shrinkage-regularized whitening (cond 1491->61). None convincingly beats baseline single-trial; full/shrink recenter HURT concept-avg (noise amplification, see perception-signal-recenter-fails). CONCLUSION: input-space per-subject alignment is NOT the cross-subject bottleneck for this learned encoder (unlike MI/workload where it was, feeding classical classifiers). The residual whitening removes isn't what breaks transfer. Next levers target the ENCODER learning a subject-invariant SEMANTIC map: (a) domain-adversarial gradient-reversal on subject id (bd 36g, embedding-space invariance); (b) bigger encoder / longer training (current 693k, early-stops ~ep9-15). recenter code kept opt-in+documented, dig complete.

Stage-3 perception single-trial top1 is a HARD ~1.6-2.3% band — extended the null-lever list (o2n/j4l, 2026-07-13). o2n: longer CBraMod-ft schedule (200ep/pat40 vs 60/12, test5) leaves single-trial top1 FLAT 2.28 vs 2.38 (best-val ep74 vs 59) — NOT schedule-limited; only concept-avg/top5 improve (25.5 vs 20.0). j4l: frozen geometry heads at 300ep — topo_cnn 1.57 <= 1.68@120ep (longer HURTS, heads overfit early), and a NEW GCN arm (electrode-adjacency message passing) is the weakest geometry head 1.07 << topo 1.57 (adjacency mixing loses to RBF-grid CNN + pos-attn). So single-trial top1 unmoved by: ft-schedule, frozen-head-schedule, GCN geometry (adds to dpi/36g/lbd/input-align nulls). Frozen heads cap ~NICE(1.6); ft ~2.3. 830 LOSO-folds+multiseed = PREMATURE RIGOR until a lever actually moves single-trial past the band (owner: against folds on a marginal/flat number).

Foundation-model encoder for Stage-3 perception (epic bji): FIRST try = CBraMod (ICLR 2025, github.com/wjq-learning/CBraMod, HF braindecode/CBraMod, BSD-3-Clause, ~4M params, flexible criss-cross patch embedding 16-256ch), NOT LaBraM despite the researcher's headline rec. Reason: the 3 integration killers (deep-dive 2026-07-09_eeg_foundation_models_for_perception) all hit LaBraM — fixed 10-20 label embeddings vs our 63 posterior ch (needs a projection adapter), z-score vs THINGS MVNN normalization, and UNSPECIFIED license. CBraMod solves all three: flexible montage (minimal adapter), BSD-3 clean, and 4M params = less overfit on 10 subjects than LaBraM. LaBraM's only edge (2534h pretraining) likely eaten by clinical->perception domain shift. LaBraM = comparison arm, not first. GREENFIELD: no published foundation-model->THINGS-EEG2 retrieval exists. Field refs (all TRIAL-AVERAGED): NICE 15.6%/42.8% within-subj, MindAlign 54.1%/83.4% within-subj + 34.4%/64.8% cross-subj LOSO. Our angle = cross-subject SINGLE-TRIAL (unreported; our NICE ~1.9%). The h1p encoder seam makes the swap cheap; low-regret call.

Stage-3 EEG->image cross-subject SINGLE-TRIAL retrieval (~1.9%, 4x chance, train4/test5) is a genuine HARD FLOOR — three mechanism-grounded levers all fail to lift it beyond noise: (1) dpi per-subject re-centering ~1.71 flat (hurts concept-avg, noise-amp on ill-cond covariance); (2) 36g domain-adversarial 2.00±0.06 3-seed (Δ+0.13 within noise, weak top5-only +0.43, ramp doesn't help); (3) lbd concept-aware soft-negative InfoNCE 1.73 (single-seed, below 1.87 baseline mean, washes). Baseline 1.87±0.14. Covers input-space alignment, embedding-space invariance, AND loss-quality — three different axes, all null. CONCLUSION: the gap is bounded by single-trial SNR / compact-encoder capacity, NOT by a fixable subject-shift or loss bug. Honest publishable negative. Untested remaining axes = ENCODER CAPACITY (bigger net/longer schedule) — a different question (representation power, not transfer). All three lever operators kept opt-in+documented in TrainConfig. Reaffirmed: multi-seed essential — single-seed hope (36g +0.32) washed.

pyriemann 0.11/0.12.dev = pure numpy (grepped installed source + docs + web: zero torch/array-api refs, deps numpy/scipy/sklearn/joblib). No GPU path without from-scratch torch reimpl of the geometry ops. Bench (numpy, BLAS-threaded): mean_riemann 22x22=36ms, 28x28=396ms, 72x72=5.9s for ~200 mats. Our riemann runs are 22-28 dim (bnci MI, eeg n-back) => already 36-400ms/fold, NOT a bottleneck; the 72-dim fnirs path uses fnirs_lda (features->LDA), never mean_riemann. Verdict: GPU-via-pyriemann = no win (no path + not the bottleneck + transfer overhead would dominate at these sizes). Deferred, do not wire.

At the FINDING stage (no working number yet), skip sweeps/ablations/multi-seed/k-fold — they are PERFECTING tools that harden an already-working result, and at finding-stage they cost huge wall-time for ~zero signal about whether a method is better. Multi-seed parity / subject-count sweeps on a near-chance result teach nothing. Right early loop: method tweak -> CHEAPEST test that shows a signal (single run, small data, fast) -> keep or kill -> next tweak. Precision AFTER a number moves. Owner's constraint is TIME-TO-DELIVER; do not spend it measuring a broken method harder. Applies across all personal_projects. Origin: I mis-set Stage-3 perception Phase-1 goal to a subject-count 'headline' sweep + multi-seed parity when cross-subject retrieval was still near-chance (~1.9%); owner correctly called it premature rigor. No SOTA paper (e.g. NICE) leads with subject-count — they chase the retrieval number via method/representation.

fNIRS-informed weighted EEG inverse (bd 4so, Liu-1998 fMRI-informed prior K=R Gᵀ(GRGᵀ+λ²I)⁻¹ with R=diag(fNIRS activation)) does NOT beat a plain inverse for cross-subject source-space workload decode — WIRED + RAN (source_prior_decode.py, Shin n-back paired EEG+fNIRS, 702 blocks/26 subj, per-subject unsupervised fNIRS HbO-magnitude prior RBF-interpolated onto fsaverage sources, 68-DK parcels, re-centered tangent). Four matched arms all within fold noise (std~0.08, SE~0.036): sensor 0.581, dSPM 0.592, uniform-prior 0.589, fNIRS-prior 0.593. Δ fNIRS−uniform +0.003, Δ fNIRS−dSPM +0.001 = clear null. The fNIRS 'where' regularizes the ill-posed inverse but does NOT inform the workload discriminant. Confirms source-space-decode-null (728) extends to the fNIRS-priored inverse: EEG+fNIRS complementarity is not decode-accessible in output space (060), plain source space (728), OR fNIRS-informed source space (4so). Operator correct + now consumed.

Frozen CBraMod perception head-search (nm5/j4l, cross-subject train[1-4]->test5, S=1/200Hz, 120ep, matched cached features). Single-trial top1 / concept top1: mean_lin 0.60/0.50 (chance floor, identical at 60+120ep) < flat_mlp 1.21/3.00 (unordered bag of 63 channel-tokens) < pos_attn 1.59/4.00 (electrode position-embedding + self-attention) < topo_cnn 1.68/4.50 (RBF-interp onto scalp grid + 2D CNN, Bashivan 2016). GEOMETRY WINS MONOTONICALLY. Two findings: (1) SPATIAL PRESERVATION is the lever not head depth/pooling (mean/attn pool=chance, keeping tokens doubles it); (2) folding by ELECTRODE GEOMETRY beats the unordered bag and the best frozen head (topo_cnn 1.68%) BEATS from-scratch NICE (1.6%) with ZERO backbone training. All geometry heads still undertrained (val climbing at ep118/120) = lower bounds. Confirms owner's intuition: head engineering on frozen features > backbone adaptation. PR #44. Positions from eeg_positions(channels()) standard_1005 unit-disk. Next (j4l): longer schedule (undertrained), GCN arm, tune grid/sigma, then LOSO.

Per-subject z-scoring is a real cross-subject transfer fix for EEG WORKLOAD band-power (the EEG analog of Riemannian re-centering for MI). Band-power is subject-idiosyncratic in ABSOLUTE scale; z-scoring each subject by its own feature mean/std (unsupervised, no labels) lifts EEG-bandpower->LDA from 0.407 raw to 0.511 (honest calibration-half) / 0.581 (transductive, uses test blocks' own stats). fNIRS does NOT benefit (0.474->0.448/0.483) - already per-epoch baseline-corrected so subject offset removed. So earlier 'EEG near-floor cross-subject regardless of feature' was WRONG - it was missing per-subject normalization. With it EEG (~0.51-0.58) >= fNIRS (~0.47), EEG is the stronger workload modality. IMPORTANT: transductive z (test-block stats) is ~7pt optimistic vs honest calib-half; report the calib-half ~0.51 as the defensible number.

Per-subject raw-signal re-centering (recenter_signals, W=M^-1/2 whitening) is NEUTRAL on the honest metric, not a clear fix — Stage-3 EEG->image, matched train4/test5 25ep: single-trial top1 UNCHANGED (diff -0.00006, noise), concept-avg WORSE (-0.025). DIAGNOSED (ruled out bug + bias): whitening output finite/O(1) std 1.7 = no numerical bug; test concepts interleaved 80-84 reps each so strided-512 M-sample is representative = no data bias. REAL cause = NOISE AMPLIFICATION: test-subject M cond=1491 (min eig 6.6e-3), full whitening equalizes ALL directions incl noise channels (~38x relative boost), injecting noise -> raises within-concept embedding dispersion -> concept-AVG (averages 80 reps) tanks while single-trial (per-rep, already noisy) stays neutral. NOT a fundamental failure of the alignment idea — the naive full-whitening conflates alignment BENEFIT with noise COST (net neutral single-trial). Fix to test: SHRINKAGE-regularized whitening (align dominant directions only, don't amplify noise floor) — could tip single-trial positive. Earlier residual-rotation hypothesis unconfirmed/secondary; noise-amplification is the measured cause. Code opt-in (TrainConfig.recenter default False).

Sign-aware coupling fix (bd 060) is physically correct but does NOT rescue output-space EEG+fNIRS fusion decode. Re-measured fused-only Riemannian (Shin nback, 702 blocks/26 subj, 3x5-fold cross-subject): acc 0.386±0.036 vs EEG-only re-centered Riemann 0.580 (Δ -0.194, fair null). Old wrong-quadrant number was 0.34-0.40 -> sign fix barely moved it. CONCLUSION: the ERD anti-correlation sign was NOT why output-space fusion fails; the MULTIPLICATIVE joint (clean EEG × weak fNIRS × coverage, drops phase/envelope) is architecturally lossy. Confirms the standing conclusion: output-space fusion adds nothing decodable; source-space/generative fusion (Stage-4, wv1 epic) is the real untested lever. 060 = correctness improvement + negative confirmation, not a decode win. Operator kept physically-correct + documented.

s1t2 (ft-vs-NICE paired delta CI, 830-without-folds): the fold-free bootstrap delta was NOT run empirically — power arithmetic makes it a poor use of GPU. n=16224 test5 trials, gap ~0.7pp (CBraMod-ft ~2.3 vs NICE ~1.6), per-arm proportion SE ~0.11pp -> paired delta ~5sigma p<1e-6 -> the test-noise CI excludes 0 near-certainly (foregone verdict). CRITICAL LIMITATION: a bootstrap over TEST TRIALS captures test-set sampling noise ONLY, not training-SEED variance, so 'real' would only mean 'beyond test noise for these two trained encoders', NOT seed-robust. The axis most likely to actually be scatter (seed-to-seed between two separately-trained encoders) is exactly what this tool CANNOT see, and multi-seed is vetoed as premature. No cached checkpoints existed -> would need 2 fresh ~40min trains to confirm a ~5sigma foregone number on the wrong axis. Decision: shipped the paired_delta CLI + Metrics.boot_ci/boot_delta_ci (tested+gated) as the deliverable, recorded the estimate, banked the wall-time. Lesson: estimate power BEFORE spending GPU; if the test-noise CI is forecastable AND the real uncertainty (seed) is out of the bootstrap's reach, the run buys confirmation not signal.

CBraMod foundation encoder on Stage-3 cross-subject perception (epic bji, matched trainer, resample200, perception_converged 60ep, chance 0.5%): frozen < NICE < FINE-TUNED, REPLICATED on 2 held-out subjects. Per-subject single-trial top1 [test5 / test1]: CBraMod-FROZEN 0.63 (probe, ~chance) < NICE-from-scratch 1.60/1.66 < CBraMod-FINE-TUNED (unfrozen, lr1e-4) 2.38/2.04. Fine-tuned beats NICE on ALL metrics BOTH subjects (8/8): single-top5 9.58/8.71 vs 7.05/7.59, concept-top1 4.5/6.0 vs 3.0/5.5, concept-top5 20.0/19.0 vs 18.0/16.5, val 7.51/6.96 vs 5.43/5.03. Mean single-top1 ~2.21 vs ~1.63 = ~1.35x NICE, consistent direction. Both ft runs UNDER-trained (best ep59/51, val still climbing) -> lower bound. VERDICT (confirmed): pretrained capacity ADAPTED is a REAL lever; the cross-subject perception wall is NOT a pure single-trial SNR floor. First method in the arc to beat the ~1.9% NICE baseline (dpi/36g/lbd all null). Frozen linear-probe FAILS (clinical TUEG features don't encode perception discriminants) — only fine-tuning cashes it. CAVEATS: 2 subjects not full 10-LOSO, single seed each, one untuned lr (backbone=head). Hardening = follow-up (LOSO multi-seed, longer schedule, discriminative-lr/LoRA, spatial head).

Domain-adversarial (bd 36g) FINAL verdict — NOT a headline lever for Stage-3 EEG->image cross-subject transfer. train4/test5 25ep. Constant w0.5 lambda1.0, 3 seeds: single-trial top1 base 1.87±0.14 vs adv 2.00±0.06 (Δ+0.13pp, WITHIN noise — single-seed +0.32 washed out); single-trial top5 base 7.75±0.17 vs adv 8.18±0.17 (Δ+0.43 ~2.5sigma, weak REAL but ranking-only); concept-avg nil. lambda-RAMP (0->1 sched, single seed): single1 1.89 / single5 7.78 = AT BASELINE, worse than constant adv — ramp does NOT rescue. Adversary acts (consistent val-down ~4.7 vs 5.2) but benefit is a marginal top5 nudge, never reaches single-trial-top1. CONCLUSION: neither input-alignment (dpi, killed) nor embedding-adversarial (36g, weak-top5-only) closes the cross-subject single-trial-top1 gap at train-4. The gap is a genuine hard ceiling for this compact encoder at this scale — subject-invariance is NOT the missing piece; likely single-trial SNR / encoder capacity. Reaffirmed: trust multi-seed over single-seed on near-chance retrieval.

Source-space EEG projection does NOT beat sensor space for cross-subject decode (bd 728, workload n-back). Ran source_decode.py: sensor (28ch) re-centered-tangent cross-subject acc 0.594±0.060 vs source (68 Desikan-Killiany parcels via fsaverage template forward + dSPM inverse) 0.602±0.063, Δ+0.008 = FAIR NULL. Template-head source localization adds regularization, not information — the sensor covariance already captures the cross-subject-transferable structure. IMPORTANT: this REFUTES the wv1 Phase-2 gate claim 'source-space gives a lever output-space lacked' — that was asserted from the 060 output-space null + the operators existing, WITHOUT running the decode. Actually run = null. Caveats (untested): individual-MRI (vs template) head models, a different decoder, or the fNIRS-prior weighted inverse (4so, unrun) could still differ. But plain source projection = no decode lever. The 728 operator is correct + now USED (source_decode), just not a decode win. Lesson: don't close a gate on 'operator built'; run the decode.
